title: AI Development Consultor
summary: Architected AI-powered automation platforms spanning user-facing applications, asynchronous workflow orchestration, and cloud delivery for contract-processing systems.
highlights: Architected and developed a React application with hybrid server/client rendering using TypeScript.
Architected AI services as a multi-agent task-based automation system using Node.js, Azure Functions, Storage Queues, and SignalR.
Improved throughput and developer experience by centralizing AI workflows and monitoring capabilities.
manual_highlights: Architected the "Unattended Mode" platform, an asynchronous work-farm architecture for AI-driven email and contract processing without requiring users to wait in front of a UI.

Designed the platform around three core services: a connector API for task intake and third-party email ingestion, a monitor application for task and flow persistence plus observability, and a queue-triggered Azure Functions worker tier for agent execution.

Modeled the system around flows and tasks, where each task carried processing data, agent configuration, and callback instructions, enabling multi-step agent workflows and structured webhook-based result delivery.

Implemented a queue-driven execution model using Azure Storage Queues and Azure Functions so tasks could be processed in parallel and the platform could scale horizontally under background workload demand.
Created an abstraction layer that made it easier for developers to add new agents and compose new workflows on top of the platform.
Implemented agents with RAG patterns via tool functions and an MCP server so AI workflows could retrieve grounded context during contract-processing tasks.
Built production applications with Codex as a primary development tool, accelerating implementation across frontend, AI service, and delivery work.
Built an AI-powered Outlook add-in to automate contract processing workflows for energy retail market users.
Built the Outlook add-in frontend in React and Next.js, delivering a complex UI for reviewing, correcting, and submitting extracted contract data.
Implemented the AI contract-analysis workflow that processed contract emails, extracted and standardized business data, and prepared it for user review and downstream submission.
Integrated the workflow so reviewed contract data could be submitted into the Enerex application database.
Provisioned and deployed Azure Functions, web applications, databases, and Storage Queues to support Enerex products.
Controlled repository quality through code reviews and pull request approvals.
Implemented deployment pipelines for application delivery and environment updates.

manual_notes.note: Enerex operates in the energy retail market and supports a suite of applications used by market participants. Built an Outlook add-in for contract-processing automation using AI: users open an email with a contract, launch the add-in, the agent analyzes the contract and returns extracted standardized data, the UI allows review and edits, and the user submits the final data into the Enerex application database.

Also worked in a DevOps capacity at Enerex, creating and deploying Azure Functions, web applications, databases, and Storage Queues, reviewing code and approving pull requests, and implementing deployment pipelines.
Used Codex at Enerex to support software development and project management.
Built applications at Enerex using Codex as a primary development workflow.
The Outlook add-in built at Enerex used React and Next.js and included a complex UI for user review and interaction.

Architected the 'Unattended Mode' system to process contract emails as background tasks. The platform used a work-farm style architecture with three main services: a connector web application exposing APIs for task intake and third-party email delivery, a monitor application with persistence and monitoring capabilities, and a queue-triggered Azure Functions worker that executed agent tasks, created follow-up tasks, or delivered final results by webhook. The system modeled all work as flows containing tasks, allowing multi-agent workflows. Azure Storage Queue messages carried task IDs, enabling inherently parallel task execution and scalable background processing. Also created abstractions so additional agents and workflows could be added easily by other developers.
Implemented agents with RAG via tool functions and an MCP server at Enerex.

source.notes: Parsed from JOSE BANDE SERRANO RESUME V6.pdf
